response:
# Towards Dynamic Benchmarks for Autonomous Materials Discovery

## JSON Output

```json
{
  "title": "Towards Dynamic Benchmarks for Autonomous Materials Discovery",
  "experiments_summary": "The paper proposes MADE (MAterials Discovery Environment), a dynamic benchmark framework for evaluating autonomous materials discovery agents. The experimental setup involves: (1) Two chemical systems tested: binary Na-O and ternary Co-Nb-Sn; (2) Oracle implementation using ORB-v3 MLIP for formation energy evaluation with FIRE optimizer relaxation (100 steps max, fmax=0.01); (3) Two structure generators: Random (sampling compositions up to 12 atoms with uniform lattice parameters) and MatterGen (pretrained model with diffusion guidance=2.0); (4) Two acquisition functions: Random selection and Diversity-based (maximizing minimum Euclidean distance in composition space); (5) Initial structures retrieved from Materials Project with two initialization modes: elemental end-members only or all known stable structures; (6) Evaluation over 20 query budget with stability threshold ε=0.01 eV/atom; (7) Novelty checking via pymatgen StructureMatcher; (8) Metrics include number of novel stable compounds discovered and number of unique structures proposed; (9) Five episodes run per configuration with standard error reported. The framework is modular, supporting different oracles (MLIP, DFT, experimental), generators, and acquisition strategies in a gym-like environment interface."
}
```
